addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	This page describes the configuration settings that the server will query from the LSP client.
2	
3	---
4	
5	## texlab.build.executable
6	
7	Defines the executable of the LaTeX build tool.
8	
9	**Type:** `string`
10	
11	**Default value:** `latexmk`
12	
13	---
14	
15	## texlab.build.args
16	
17	Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured LaTeX build tool.
18	Note that flags and their arguments need to be separate
19	elements in this array.
20	To pass the arguments `-foo bar` to a build tool,
21	`latex.build.args` needs to be `["-foo", "bar"]`.
22	The placeholder `%f` will be replaced by the server.
23	
24	**Placeholders:**
25	
26	- `%f`: The path of the TeX file to compile.
27	
28	**Type:** `string[]`
29	
30	**Default value:** `["-pdf", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "-synctex=1", "%f"]`
31	
32	---
33	
34	## texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter
35	
36	Set this property to `true` if you want to execute a forward search after a build.
37	
38	**Type:** `boolean`
39	
40	**Default value:** `false`
41	
42	---
43	
44	## texlab.build.onSave
45	
46	Set this property to `true` if you want to compile the project after saving a file.
47	
48	**Type:** `boolean`
49	
50	**Default value:** `false`
51	
52	---
53	
54	## texlab.build.useFileList
55	
56	When set to `true`, the server will use the `.fls` files produced by the TeX engine as an additional input for the project detection.
57	
58	Note that enabling this property might have an impact on performance.
59	
60	**Type:** `boolean`
61	
62	**Default value:** `false`
63	
64	---
65	
66	## texlab.build.auxDirectory
67	
68	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the `.aux` files.
69	Note that you need to set the aux directory in `latex.build.args` too.
70	
71	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
72	
73	**Type:** `string`
74	
75	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
76	
77	---
78	
79	## texlab.build.logDirectory
80	
81	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the build log files.
82	Note that you need to change the output directory in your build arguments too.
83	
84	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
85	
86	**Type:** `string`
87	
88	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
89	
90	---
91	
92	## texlab.build.pdfDirectory
93	
94	When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the output files.
95	Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too.
96	
97	When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.
98	
99	**Type:** `string`
100	
101	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)
102	
103	---
104	
105	## texlab.build.filename
106	
107	Allows overriding the default file name of the build artifact. 
108	This setting is used to find the correct PDF file to open during forward search.
109	
110	**Type:** `string`
111	
112	**Default value:** `null` (by default, the server searches for `foo.pdf` if the main document is named `foo.tex`)
113	
114	---
115	
116	
117	## (DEPRECATED) texlab.auxDirectory
118	
119	Defines the directory containing the build artifacts.
120	Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too,
121	if you want to change the build directory.
122	In this case, use the `-outdir` flag for `latexmk`.
123	
124	**Type:** `string`
125	
126	**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the TeX file)
127	
128	---
129	
130	## texlab.forwardSearch.executable
131	
132	Defines the executable of the PDF previewer.
133	The previewer needs to support [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf).
134	
135	**Type:** `string | null`
136	
137	**Default value:** `null`
138	
139	---
140	
141	## texlab.forwardSearch.args
142	
143	Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured previewer to perform the forward search.
144	The placeholders `%f, %p, %l` will be replaced by the server.
145	
146	**Placeholders:**
147	
148	- `%f`: The path of the current TeX file.
149	- `%p`: The path of the current PDF file.
150	- `%l`: The current line number.
151	
152	**Type:** `string[] | null`
153	
154	**Default value:** `null`
155	
156	---
157	
158	## texlab.chktex.onOpenAndSave
159	
160	Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after opening and saving a file.
161	
162	**Type:** `boolean`
163	
164	**Default value:** `false`
165	
166	---
167	
168	## texlab.chktex.onEdit
169	
170	Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
171	
172	**Type:** `boolean`
173	
174	**Default value:** `false`
175	
176	---
177	
178	## texlab.chktex.additionalArgs
179	
180	Additional command line arguments that are passed to [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
181	Don't redefine the `-I` and `-f` flags as they are set by the server.
182	
183	**Type:** `string[]`
184	
185	**Default value:** `[]`
186	
187	---
188	
189	## texlab.diagnosticsDelay
190	
191	Delay in milliseconds before reporting diagnostics.
192	
193	**Type:** `integer`
194	
195	**Default value:** `300`
196	
197	---
198	
199	## texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns
200	
201	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
202	If specified, only diagnostics that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
203	are sent to the client.
204	
205	See also [`texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsignoredpatterns).
206	
207	_Hint_:
208	If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
209	then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.
210	
211	**Type:** `string[]`
212	
213	**Default value:** `[]`
214	
215	---
216	
217	## texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns
218	
219	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
220	If specified, only diagnostics that match _none_ of the specified patterns
221	are sent to the client.
222	
223	See also [`texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsallowedpatterns).
224	
225	**Type:** `string[]`
226	
227	**Default value:** `[]`
228	
229	---
230	
231	## texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns
232	
233	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
234	If specified, only symbols that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
235	are sent to the client. 
236	Symbols are filtered recursively so nested symbols can still be sent to the client even though the parent node is removed from the results.
237	
238	See also [`texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsignoredpatterns).
239	
240	_Hint_:
241	If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
242	then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.
243	
244	**Type:** `string[]`
245	
246	**Default value:** `[]`
247	
248	---
249	
250	## texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns
251	
252	A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
253	If specified, only symbols that match _none_ of the specified patterns
254	are sent to the client.
255	
256	See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).
257	
258	**Type:** `string[]`
259	
260	**Default value:** `[]`
261	
262	---
263	
264	## texlab.symbols.customEnvironments
265	
266	A list of objects that allows extending the list of environments that are part of the document symbols.
267	
268	See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).
269	
270	**Type:** `SymbolEnvironmentOptions[]`
271	
272	```ts
273	interface SymbolEnvironmentOptions {
274	    // The name of the environment.
275	    name: string;
276	
277	    // The name shown in the document symbols. By default, title case is used.
278	    displayName?: string;
279	
280	    // If set, the server will try to match a label to environment and append its number.
281	    label?: false;
282	}
283	```
284	
285	**Default value:** `[]`
286	
287	---
288	
289	## texlab.formatterLineLength
290	
291	Defines the maximum amount of characters per line (0 = disable) when formatting BibTeX files.
292	
293	**Type:** `integer`
294	
295	**Default value:** `80`
296	
297	---
298	
299	## texlab.bibtexFormatter
300	
301	Defines the formatter to use for BibTeX formatting.
302	Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent` or `none`.
303	
304	**Type:** `string`
305	
306	**Default value:** `texlab`
307	
308	---
309	
310	## texlab.latexFormatter
311	
312	Defines the formatter to use for LaTeX formatting.
313	Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent`, `tex-fmt` or `none`.
314	Note that `texlab` is not implemented yet.
315	
316	**Type:** `string`
317	
318	**Default value:** `latexindent`
319	
320	---
321	
322	## texlab.latexindent.local
323	
324	Defines the path of a file containing the `latexindent` configuration.
325	This corresponds to the `--local=file.yaml` flag of `latexindent`.
326	By default the configuration inside the project root directory is used.
327	
328	**Type:** `string`
329	
330	**Default value:** `null`
331	
332	---
333	
334	## texlab.latexindent.modifyLineBreaks
335	
336	Modifies linebreaks before, during, and at the end of code blocks
337	when formatting with `latexindent`.
338	This corresponds to the `--modifylinebreaks` flag of `latexindent`.
339	
340	**Type:** `boolean`
341	
342	**Default value:** `false`
343	
344	---
345	
346	## texlab.latexindent.replacement
347	
348	Defines an additional replacement flag that is added when calling `latexindent`.
349	This can be one of the following:
350	- `"-r"` 
351	- `"-rv"` 
352	- `"-rr"`
353	- `null`
354	
355	By default no replacement flag is passed.
356	
357	**Type:** string
358	
359	**Default value:** `null`
360	
361	---
362	
363	## texlab.completion.matcher
364	
365	Modifies the algorithm used to filter the completion items returned to the client.
366	Possibles values are:
367	- `fuzzy`: Fuzzy string matching (case sensitive)
368	- `fuzzy-ignore-case`: Fuzzy string matching (case insensitive)
369	- `prefix`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case sensitive)
370	- `prefix-ignore-case`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case insensitive)
371	
372	**Type:** `string`
373	
374	**Default value:** `fuzzy-ignore-case`
375	
376	---
377	
378	## texlab.hover.symbols
379	
380	Modifies the way symbol-like commands (e. g. `\epsilon`) are displayed during a hover request.
381	Possible values are:
382	- `none`: No hover is shown
383	- `glyph`: If available, the command is shown using a unicode character
384	- `image`: If available, a markdown image preview is returned. If not, the `glyph` method is tried next.
385	
386	**Type:** `string`
387	
388	**Default value:** `image`
389	
390	---
391	
392	
393	## texlab.inlayHints.labelDefinitions
394	
395	When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\label`-like commands.
396	
397	**Type:** `boolean`
398	
399	**Default value:** `true`
400	
401	---
402	
403	## texlab.inlayHints.labelReferences
404	
405	When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\ref`-like commands.
406	
407	**Type:** `boolean`
408	
409	**Default value:** `true`
410	
411	---
412	
413	## texlab.inlayHints.maxLength
414	
415	When set, the server will truncate the text of the inlay hints to the specified length.
416	
417	**Type:** `int | null`
418	
419	**Default value:** `null`
420	
421	---
422	
423	## texlab.experimental.followPackageLinks
424	
425	If set to `true`, dependencies of custom packages are resolved and included in the dependency graph.
426	
427	**Type:** `boolean`
428	
429	**Default value:** `false`
430	
431	---
432	
433	## texlab.experimental.mathEnvironments
434	
435	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as math environments (for example `align*` or `equation`).
436	
437	**Type:** `string[]`
438	
439	**Default value:** `[]`
440	
441	---
442	
443	## texlab.experimental.enumEnvironments
444	
445	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as enumeration environments (for example `enumerate` or `itemize`).
446	
447	**Type:** `string[]`
448	
449	**Default value:** `[]`
450	
451	---
452	
453	## texlab.experimental.verbatimEnvironments
454	
455	Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as verbatim environments (for example `minted` or `lstlisting`).
456	This can be used to suppress diagnostics from environments that do not contain LaTeX code.
457	
458	**Type:** `string[]`
459	
460	**Default value:** `[]`
461	
462	---
463	
464	## texlab.experimental.citationCommands
465	
466	Allows extending the list of commands which the server considers as citation commands (for example `\cite`). 
467	
468	 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).
469	
470	**Type:** `string[]`
471	
472	**Default value:** `[]`
473	
474	---
475	
476	## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands
477	
478	Allows extending the list of `\label`-like commands.
479	
480	 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).
481	
482	**Type:** `string[]`
483	
484	**Default value:** `[]`
485	
486	---
487	
488	## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceCommands
489	
490	Allows extending the list of `\ref`-like commands.
491	
492	 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).
493	
494	**Type:** `string[]`
495	
496	**Default value:** `[]`
497	
498	---
499	
500	## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceRangeCommands
501	
502	Allows extending the list of `\crefrange`-like commands.
503	
504	 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).
505	
506	**Type:** `string[]`
507	
508	**Default value:** `[]`
509	
510	---
511	
512	## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes
513	
514	Allows associating a label definition command with a custom prefix.
515	Consider,
516	
517	```tex
518	\newcommand{\thm}[1]{\label{thm:#1}}
519	\thm{foo}
520	```
521	
522	Then setting `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes` to `[["thm", "thm:"]]` and adding "thm" to `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands`

This page describes the configuration settings that the server will query from the LSP client.

---

## texlab.build.executable

Defines the executable of the LaTeX build tool.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `latexmk`

---

## texlab.build.args

Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured LaTeX build tool.
Note that flags and their arguments need to be separate
elements in this array.
To pass the arguments `-foo bar` to a build tool,
`latex.build.args` needs to be `["-foo", "bar"]`.
The placeholder `%f` will be replaced by the server.

**Placeholders:**

- `%f`: The path of the TeX file to compile.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `["-pdf", "-interaction=nonstopmode", "-synctex=1", "%f"]`

---

## texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter

Set this property to `true` if you want to execute a forward search after a build.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.onSave

Set this property to `true` if you want to compile the project after saving a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.useFileList

When set to `true`, the server will use the `.fls` files produced by the TeX engine as an additional input for the project detection.

Note that enabling this property might have an impact on performance.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.build.auxDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the `.aux` files.
Note that you need to set the aux directory in `latex.build.args` too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.logDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the build log files.
Note that you need to change the output directory in your build arguments too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.pdfDirectory

When not using `latexmk`, provides a way to define the directory containing the output files.
Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too.

When using a `latexmkrc` file, `texlab` will automatically infer the correct setting.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the root directory)

---

## texlab.build.filename

Allows overriding the default file name of the build artifact. 
This setting is used to find the correct PDF file to open during forward search.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `null` (by default, the server searches for `foo.pdf` if the main document is named `foo.tex`)

---


## (DEPRECATED) texlab.auxDirectory

Defines the directory containing the build artifacts.
Note that you need to set the output directory in `latex.build.args` too,
if you want to change the build directory.
In this case, use the `-outdir` flag for `latexmk`.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `.` (the same directory as the TeX file)

---

## texlab.forwardSearch.executable

Defines the executable of the PDF previewer.
The previewer needs to support [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf).

**Type:** `string | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.forwardSearch.args

Defines additional arguments that are passed to the configured previewer to perform the forward search.
The placeholders `%f, %p, %l` will be replaced by the server.

**Placeholders:**

- `%f`: The path of the current TeX file.
- `%p`: The path of the current PDF file.
- `%l`: The current line number.

**Type:** `string[] | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.chktex.onOpenAndSave

Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after opening and saving a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.chktex.onEdit

Lint using [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.chktex.additionalArgs

Additional command line arguments that are passed to [chktex](https://www.nongnu.org/chktex/) after editing a file.
Don't redefine the `-I` and `-f` flags as they are set by the server.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.diagnosticsDelay

Delay in milliseconds before reporting diagnostics.

**Type:** `integer`

**Default value:** `300`

---

## texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
If specified, only diagnostics that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsignoredpatterns).

_Hint_:
If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.diagnostics.ignoredPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported diagnostics.
If specified, only diagnostics that match _none_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.diagnostics.allowedPatterns`](#texlabdiagnosticsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
If specified, only symbols that match _at least one_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client. 
Symbols are filtered recursively so nested symbols can still be sent to the client even though the parent node is removed from the results.

See also [`texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsignoredpatterns).

_Hint_:
If both `allowedPatterns` and `ignoredPatterns` are set,
then allowed patterns are applied first. Afterwards, the results are filtered with the ignored patterns.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.ignoredPatterns

A list of regular expressions used to filter the list of reported document symbols.
If specified, only symbols that match _none_ of the specified patterns
are sent to the client.

See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.symbols.customEnvironments

A list of objects that allows extending the list of environments that are part of the document symbols.

See also [`texlab.symbols.allowedPatterns`](#texlabsymbolsallowedpatterns).

**Type:** `SymbolEnvironmentOptions[]`

```ts
interface SymbolEnvironmentOptions {
    // The name of the environment.
    name: string;

    // The name shown in the document symbols. By default, title case is used.
    displayName?: string;

    // If set, the server will try to match a label to environment and append its number.
    label?: false;
}
```

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.formatterLineLength

Defines the maximum amount of characters per line (0 = disable) when formatting BibTeX files.

**Type:** `integer`

**Default value:** `80`

---

## texlab.bibtexFormatter

Defines the formatter to use for BibTeX formatting.
Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent` or `none`.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `texlab`

---

## texlab.latexFormatter

Defines the formatter to use for LaTeX formatting.
Possible values are either `texlab`, `latexindent`, `tex-fmt` or `none`.
Note that `texlab` is not implemented yet.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `latexindent`

---

## texlab.latexindent.local

Defines the path of a file containing the `latexindent` configuration.
This corresponds to the `--local=file.yaml` flag of `latexindent`.
By default the configuration inside the project root directory is used.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.latexindent.modifyLineBreaks

Modifies linebreaks before, during, and at the end of code blocks
when formatting with `latexindent`.
This corresponds to the `--modifylinebreaks` flag of `latexindent`.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.latexindent.replacement

Defines an additional replacement flag that is added when calling `latexindent`.
This can be one of the following:
- `"-r"` 
- `"-rv"` 
- `"-rr"`
- `null`

By default no replacement flag is passed.

**Type:** string

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.completion.matcher

Modifies the algorithm used to filter the completion items returned to the client.
Possibles values are:
- `fuzzy`: Fuzzy string matching (case sensitive)
- `fuzzy-ignore-case`: Fuzzy string matching (case insensitive)
- `prefix`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case sensitive)
- `prefix-ignore-case`: Filter out items that do not start with the search text (case insensitive)

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `fuzzy-ignore-case`

---

## texlab.hover.symbols

Modifies the way symbol-like commands (e. g. `\epsilon`) are displayed during a hover request.
Possible values are:
- `none`: No hover is shown
- `glyph`: If available, the command is shown using a unicode character
- `image`: If available, a markdown image preview is returned. If not, the `glyph` method is tried next.

**Type:** `string`

**Default value:** `image`

---


## texlab.inlayHints.labelDefinitions

When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\label`-like commands.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `true`

---

## texlab.inlayHints.labelReferences

When enabled, the server will return inlay hints for `\ref`-like commands.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `true`

---

## texlab.inlayHints.maxLength

When set, the server will truncate the text of the inlay hints to the specified length.

**Type:** `int | null`

**Default value:** `null`

---

## texlab.experimental.followPackageLinks

If set to `true`, dependencies of custom packages are resolved and included in the dependency graph.

**Type:** `boolean`

**Default value:** `false`

---

## texlab.experimental.mathEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as math environments (for example `align*` or `equation`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.enumEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as enumeration environments (for example `enumerate` or `itemize`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.verbatimEnvironments

Allows extending the list of environments which the server considers as verbatim environments (for example `minted` or `lstlisting`).
This can be used to suppress diagnostics from environments that do not contain LaTeX code.

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.citationCommands

Allows extending the list of commands which the server considers as citation commands (for example `\cite`). 

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands

Allows extending the list of `\label`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceCommands

Allows extending the list of `\ref`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferenceRangeCommands

Allows extending the list of `\crefrange`-like commands.

 _Hint:_ Additional commands need to be written **without** a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type:** `string[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes

Allows associating a label definition command with a custom prefix.
Consider,

```tex
\newcommand{\thm}[1]{\label{thm:#1}}
\thm{foo}
```

Then setting `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes` to `[["thm", "thm:"]]` and adding "thm" to `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionCommands` 
will make the server recognize the `thm:foo` label.

**Type:** `(string, string)[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.labelReferencePrefixes

Allows associating a label reference command with a custom prefix.
See `texlab.experimental.labelDefinitionPrefixes` for more details.

**Type:** `(string, string)[]`

**Default value:** `[]`

---

## texlab.experimental.glossaryReferenceCommands

Allows extending the list of `\gls`-like commands.

_Hint_: Additional commands need to be written without a leading `\` (e. g. `foo` instead of `\foo`).

**Type**: `string[]`

**Default value**: `[]`

----

# Custom Messages

We extend the [Language Server Protocol](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification)
with custom messages to provide better LaTeX integration.
These messages are _optional_ and it is up to the client to support them.

## Build Request

The build request is sent from the client to the server to build a given LaTeX document.
A `textDocumentBuild` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.

_Request_:

- method: 'textDocument/build'
- params: `BuildTextDocumentParams` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface BuildTextDocumentParams {
  /**
   * The text document to build.
   */
  textDocument: TextDocumentIdentifier;

  /**
   * The position of the cursor for use in forward search (optional).
   */
  position?: Position;
}
```

_Response_:

- result: `BuildResult` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface BuildResult {
  /**
   * The status of the build process.
   */
  status: BuildStatus;
}

enum BuildStatus {
  /**
   * The build process terminated without any errors.
   */
  Success = 0,

  /**
   * The build process terminated with errors.
   */
  Error = 1,

  /**
   * The build process failed to start or crashed.
   */
  Failure = 2,

  /**
   * The build process was cancelled.
   */
  Cancelled = 3,
}
```

## Forward Search Request

The forward search request is sent from the client to the server when the user requests a forward search via SyncTeX.
A `textDocumentForwardSearch` experimental capability is used to indicate the presence of this custom request.

_Request_:

- method: 'textDocument/forwardSearch'
- params: [`TextDocumentPositionParams`](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specification#textdocumentpositionparams)

_Response_:

- result: `ForwardSearchResult` defined as follows:

```typescript
interface ForwardSearchResult {
  /**
   * The status of the previewer process.
   */
  status: ForwardSearchStatus;
}

enum ForwardSearchStatus {
  /**
   * The previewer process executed the command without any errors.
   */
  Success = 0,

  /**
   * The previewer process executed the command with errors.
   */
  Error = 1,

  /**
   * The previewer process failed to start or crashed.
   */
  Failure = 2,

  /**
   * The previewer command is not configured.
   */
  Unconfigured = 3,
}
```

# Enum Mapping

The following table describes the mapping of LaTeX and BibTeX structures
to their `CompletionItemKind` and `SymbolKind`.

| LaTeX / BibTeX structure  | CompletionItemKind   | SymbolKind           |
| ------------------------- | -------------------- | -------------------- |
| Command                   | `Function` (3)       | `Function` (12)      |
| Command Argument          | `Value` (12)         | `Number` (16)        |
| Command Definition        |                      | `Key` (20)           |
| Snippet                   | `Snippet` (15)       |                      |
| Environment               | `Enum` (13)          | `Enum` (10)          |
| Section                   | `Module` (9)         | `Module` (2)         |
| Float                     | `Method` (2)         | `Method` (6)         |
| Theorem                   | `Variable` (6)       | `Variable` (13)      |
| Equation                  | `Constant` (21)      | `Constant` (14)      |
| Enumeration Item          | `EnumMember` (20)    | `EnumMember` (22)    |
| Label                     | `Constructor` (4)    | `Constructor` (9)    |
| Folder                    | `Folder` (19)        | `Namespace` (3)      |
| File                      | `File` (17)          | `File` (1)           |
| PGF Library               | `Property` (10)      | `Property` (7)       |
| TikZ Library              | `Property` (10)      | `Property` (7)       |
| Color                     | `Color` (16)         |                      |
| Color Model               | `Color` (16)         |                      |
| Package                   | `Class` (7)          | `Class` (5)          |
| Class                     | `Class` (7)          | `Class` (5)          |
| BibTeX Entry (Misc)       | `Interface` (8)      | `Interface` (11)     |
| BibTeX Entry (Article)    | `Event` (23)         | `Event` (24)         |
| BibTeX Entry (Book)       | `Struct` (22)        | `Struct` (23)        |
| BibTeX Entry (Collection) | `TypeParameter` (25) | `TypeParameter` (26) |
| BibTeX Entry (Part)       | `Operator` (24)      | `Operator` (25)      |
| BibTeX Entry (Thesis)     | `Unit` (11)          | `Object` (19)        |
| BibTeX String             | `Text` (1)           | `String` (15)        |
| BibTeX Field              | `Field` (5)          | `Field` (8)          |

1	`texlab` supports compiling LaTeX using a custom request (`textDocument/build`)
2	and by building a document after saving if configured to do so.
3	To enable building on save, simply set `texlab.build.onSave` to true.
4	Previewing can be configured in a variety of ways:
5	
6	1. If you are using `latexmk`, you can create a `.latexmkrc` file and call your viewer accordingly.
7	   Afterwards, you can add the `-pv` flag to your `texlab.build.args`.
8	
9	2. If you want the PDF viewer to stay synchronized with the cursor position in your editor,
10	   you can instruct `texlab` to execute a forward search after every build (`texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter`).
11	   To do so, you need to enable [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf)
12	   and update the `texlab.forwardSearch` configuration.
13	   If you want to use this feature, we do _not_ recommend the `-pvc` flag
14	   because `texlab` does not get notified by `latexmk` when a document gets built.
15	   Instead, you can use `texlab.build.onSave`.
16	
17	In the following sections, we will give SyncTeX configurations for several popular viewers.
18	If your viewer is not listed here, you can send us a pull request or create an issue.
19	
20	
21	If you editor supports the `window/showDocument` request, 
22	then you can use `texlab inverse-search --input <FILE> --line <LINE>` to forward the inverse search to the editor.
23	
24	In this section, we will give instructions for the `texlab inverse-search` subcommand. 
25	If your editor does not support it or the path of `texlab` is not fixed/known, then you need to adapt the commands to your editor.
26	Some plugins can help with setting up inverse search:
27	
28	- `Neovim`: [`f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig`](https://github.com/f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig)
29	- `Emacs`: [`ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex`](https://github.com/ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex#inverse-search)
30	
31	---
32	
33	## SumatraPDF
34	
35	We highly recommend [SumatraPDF](https://www.sumatrapdfreader.org) on Windows
36	because Adobe Reader locks the opened PDF file and will therefore prevent further builds.
37	
38	### Forward Search
39

`texlab` supports compiling LaTeX using a custom request (`textDocument/build`)
and by building a document after saving if configured to do so.
To enable building on save, simply set `texlab.build.onSave` to true.
Previewing can be configured in a variety of ways:

1. If you are using `latexmk`, you can create a `.latexmkrc` file and call your viewer accordingly.
   Afterwards, you can add the `-pv` flag to your `texlab.build.args`.

2. If you want the PDF viewer to stay synchronized with the cursor position in your editor,
   you can instruct `texlab` to execute a forward search after every build (`texlab.build.forwardSearchAfter`).
   To do so, you need to enable [SyncTeX](http://www.tug.org/TUGboat/tb29-3/tb93laurens.pdf)
   and update the `texlab.forwardSearch` configuration.
   If you want to use this feature, we do _not_ recommend the `-pvc` flag
   because `texlab` does not get notified by `latexmk` when a document gets built.
   Instead, you can use `texlab.build.onSave`.

In the following sections, we will give SyncTeX configurations for several popular viewers.
If your viewer is not listed here, you can send us a pull request or create an issue.


If you editor supports the `window/showDocument` request, 
then you can use `texlab inverse-search --input <FILE> --line <LINE>` to forward the inverse search to the editor.

In this section, we will give instructions for the `texlab inverse-search` subcommand. 
If your editor does not support it or the path of `texlab` is not fixed/known, then you need to adapt the commands to your editor.
Some plugins can help with setting up inverse search:

- `Neovim`: [`f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig`](https://github.com/f3fora/nvim-texlabconfig)
- `Emacs`: [`ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex`](https://github.com/ROCKTAKEY/lsp-latex#inverse-search)

---

## SumatraPDF

We highly recommend [SumatraPDF](https://www.sumatrapdfreader.org) on Windows
because Adobe Reader locks the opened PDF file and will therefore prevent further builds.

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "C:/Users/{User}/AppData/Local/SumatraPDF/SumatraPDF.exe",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": [
    "-reuse-instance",
    "%p",
    "-forward-search",
    "%f",
    "%l"
  ]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Add the following line to your SumatraPDF settings file (Menu -> Settings -> Advanced Options):

```ini
InverseSearchCmdLine = "texlab inverse-search --input "%f" --line %l"
```

You can execute the search by pressing `Alt+DoubleClick` in the PDF document.

---

## Evince

The SyncTeX feature of [Evince](https://wiki.gnome.org/Apps/Evince) requires communication via D-Bus.
In order to use it from the command line, install the [evince-synctex](https://github.com/latex-lsp/evince-synctex) script.

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "evince-synctex",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["-f", "%l", "%p", "\"texlab -i %f -l %l\""]
}
```

### Inverse Search

The inverse search feature is already configured if you use `evince-synctex`.
You can execute the search by pressing `Ctrl+Click` in the PDF document.

---

## Okular

### Forward Search

Add the following lines to your editor config:

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "okular",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--unique", "file:%p#src:%l%f"]
}
```

### Inverse Search

Change the editor of Okular (Settings -> Configure Okular... -> Editor) to "Custom Text Editor"
and set the editor command.
For `texlab inverse-search`, you can use the following command:

```bash
texlab inverse-search -i "%f" -l %l
```

You can execute the search by pressing `Shift+Click` in the PDF document.

## Zathura

### Forward Search

```json
{
  "texlab.forwardSearch.executable": "zathura",
  "texlab.forwardSearch.args": ["--synctex-forward", "%l:1:%f", "%p"]

```

file.content:
# Discovery

Whenever you open a TeX file in the editor, `texlab` tries to find out all files that are part of the same _project_, meaning they are compiled into the same document. The server needs this information to implement most of its features. For example, packages imported in the preamble should be visible in other project files as well. In particular, the project is also used determine the root document, which is passed to the TeX engine when compiling.

## Algorithm

The server does the following steps to figure out the project:
- Walk up the directory tree until we either reach the file system root (`/`), the home directory or a root directory (see below).
  For each directory along the way, we load all contained TeX files that we have not opened yet.
- Next, we build a dependency tree by resolving include commands such as `\input` or `\import`.
  The referenced files are added to the workspace.
- Repeat this procedure until there are no more changes to the workspace

# Root directory

Due to the way, the TeX engine is designed, commands such as `\input` operate on the working directory of the TeX engine instead on the directory of the parent source file. That's why we need to determine the root directory in the server. 
`texlab` walks up the directory tree of the document and tries the following steps in this order:

1. Check if there is a `.texlabroot`/`texlabroot` file. Then, the server uses the 
`texlab.build.auxDirectory`, `texlab.build.logDirectory` and `texlab.build.pdfDirectory` settings to find the build artifacts and auxiliary files.
2. Check if there is a `Tectonic.toml` manifest. Then, the server uses the `src` and `build` directories and adds `_preamble.tex` and `_postamble.tex` to the project (if present).
3. Check if there is a `.latexmkrc`/`latexmkrc` file. Then, the server uses the settings defined inside the `latexmkrc`. If failing to do so, then `texlab.build.auxDirectory`, `texlab.build.logDirectory` and `texlab.build.pdfDirectory` are used as fallback.
4. Use the directory of the root source file. A root source file is a TeX document that has a `document` environment. Documents with `\documentclass{subfiles}` are excluded.

[`tectonic`](https://tectonic-typesetting.github.io/) is a modernized, alternative TeX engine.
Most features of `texlab` work out of the box when using `tectonic`.
To compile documents through `texlab`, you need to change the configuration.
See `tectonic --help` for more information about the flags.

---

**Hint:**

Please make sure to set `texlab.build.pdfDirectory` and `texlab.build.auxDirectory` to the configured output directory (see `--outdir` argument).

Also, `--keep-intermediates` is recommended because they allow `texlab`
to find out the section numbers and show them in the completion.
Without the `--keep-logs` flag, `texlab` won't be able to report compilation warnings.

---

## V2 CLI

```json
{
  "texlab.build.executable": "tectonic",
  "texlab.build.args": [
    "-X",
    "compile",
    "%f",
    "--synctex",
    "--keep-logs",
    "--keep-intermediates"
  ],
  "texlab.build.pdfDirectory": "build",
  "texlab.build.auxDirectory": "build"
}
```

## V1 CLI

```json
{
  "texlab.build.executable": "tectonic",
  "texlab.build.args": [
    "%f",
    "--synctex",
    "--keep-logs",
    "--keep-intermediates"
  ],
  "texlab.build.pdfDirectory": "build",
  "texlab.build.auxDirectory": "build"
}
```

The server provides the some commands through the `workspace/executeCommand` request.

__Note:__
For simplicity, all commands take at most one argument, that is, the `arguments` array should contain exactly one element or no elements depending on the command.

## texlab.cleanAuxiliary

Removes the auxiliary files produced by compiling the specified LaTeX document.
At the moment, this command simply calls `latexmk -c` with the currently configured output directory.

**Parameters**

```ts
export type CleanAuxiliaryParams = TextDocumentIdentifier;
```

## texlab.cleanArtifacts

Removes the auxiliary files and the artifacts produced by compiling the specified LaTeX document.
At the moment, this command simply calls `latexmk -C` with the currently configured output directory.

**Parameters**

```ts
export type CleanArtifactsParams = TextDocumentIdentifier;
```

## texlab.changeEnvironment

Upon receiving a `newName` through the `params`, changes the name of the inner-most environment that contains the specified position.
 
**Parameters**

```ts
export interface ChangeEnvironmentParams extends TextDocumentPositionParams {
    newName: string;
}
```

## texlab.findEnvironments

Returns a list of all environments that contain the specified position.
 
**Parameters**

```ts
export type FindEnvironmentsParams = TextDocumentPositionParams;
```

**Returns**

`EnvironmentLocation[]`, where

```ts
export interface EnvironmentLocation {
    name: {
        text: string;
        range: Range;
    };
    fullRange: Range;
}
```

## texlab.showDependencyGraph

Returns a description of the dependency graph in DOT format.

**Parameters**

None

`string` (A description of the graph in [`DOT`](https://graphviz.org/doc/info/lang.html) format)

## texlab.cancelBuild

Cancels all currently active build requests (including builds triggered by `texlab.build.onSave`)

**Parameters**

None

Now I have a complete understanding of the project. This is the **texlab.wiki** — the GitHub wiki for the `texlab` LaTeX Language Server project. It's a documentation repository, not a code repository.

Let me summarize the content:

1. **Home.md** — Just a welcome page ("Welcome to the texlab wiki!")

2. **Configuration.md** — The biggest and most detailed page. Documents all `texlab.*` configuration settings that the LSP server queries from the client. Organized into logical groups:
   - `texlab.build.*` — Build tool configuration (executable, args, onSave, forwardSearchAfter, useFileList, auxDirectory, logDirectory, pdfDirectory, filename). Plus deprecated `texlab.auxDirectory`.
   - `texlab.forwardSearch.*` — PDF previewer / forward search config (executable, args with placeholders %f, %p, %l)
   - `texlab.chktex.*` — Chktex linting (onOpenAndSave, onEdit, additionalArgs)
   - `texlab.diagnostics*` — diagnosticsDelay, diagnostics.allowedPatterns, diagnostics.ignoredPatterns
   - `texlab.symbols.*` — symbol filtering & custom environments (allowedPatterns, ignoredPatterns, customEnvironments)
   - Formatters — formatterLineLength, bibtexFormatter, latexFormatter, latexindent.* (local, modifyLineBreaks, replacement)
   - `texlab.completion.matcher` — completion matching algorithm
   - `texlab.hover.symbols` — hover display of symbols
   - `texlab.inlayHints.*` — labelDefinitions, labelReferences, maxLength
   - `texlab.experimental.*` — experimental extensions (followPackageLinks, mathEnvironments, enumEnvironments, verbatimEnvironments, citationCommands, labelDefinitionCommands, labelReferenceCommands, labelReferenceRangeCommands, labelDefinitionPrefixes, labelReferencePrefixes, glossaryReferenceCommands)

3. **LSP-Internals.md** — Documents the custom LSP messages that extend the Language Server Protocol:
   - Build Request (`textDocument/build`)
   - Forward Search Request (`textDocument/forwardSearch`)
   - Enum Mapping table (LaTeX/BibTeX structures → CompletionItemKind/SymbolKind)

4. **Previewing.md** — Configuring compiling and previewing:
   - Building on save, forward search after build
   - `texlab inverse-search` subcommand
   - SyncTeX configurations for various viewers: SumatraPDF, Evince, Okular, Zathura, qpdfview, Skim, Sioyek (forward + inverse search for each)

5. **Project-Detection.md** — How texlab discovers the project / root directory:
   - Discovery algorithm (walk up dir tree, build dependency tree, resolve \input/\import)

input.rationale: 本仓库是 texlab（LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器）的官方 wiki，共 7 个页面，本身就是文档而非源码，因此讲义的“源码”即这 7 篇 wiki，实践任务以“写配置/搭集成/调用命令/验证行为”为主。学习顺序按“先建立全局认知，再深入配置与集成，最后理解协议扩展与可扩展点”设计：单元 1 用 Home 与 Project-Detection 让读者先搞清 texlab 是什么、如何识别一个 LaTeX 工程（这是几乎所有功能的前置）；单元 2 引入配置模型（命名空间、类型、占位符）这一后续所有讲义的公共语言，并聚焦最常用的构建配置；单元 3 围绕“编译→预览”主链路展开，涵盖编译流程、SyncTeX 双向搜索与各阅读器集成、以及 tectonic 替代引擎；单元 4 进入 LSP 协议层，讲清 texlab 的自定义请求、workspace 命令与结构→符号种类映射；单元 5 面向高级配置与二次开发，把 Configuration.md 中较零散的诊断/符号/补全/格式化/inlay hints/experimental 扩展点按主题聚合。讲义依赖遵循“先懂配置模型才能读具体项”“先懂项目识别才能讲依赖图与标签前缀”“先懂编译流程才能讲自定义 build 请求”等关系，避免读者跳读时缺失前置概念。Configuration.md 内容庞大（约 40 项设置横跨构建/诊断/符号/格式化等多个子系统），故按子系统拆成多篇，而非一篇过载。

units.title: 初识 texlab：定位与项目模型

lectures.title: texlab 是什么：项目定位与 wiki 导览
lectures.topic: 介绍 texlab 是一个为编辑器提供 LaTeX/BibTeX 智能能力的语言服务器，采用 LSP 客户端-服务器架构；并导览本 wiki 的 7 个页面分别讲什么，帮助读者建立全局认知。
learning_goals: 理解 texlab 的定位：基于 LSP 的 LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器
掌握 LSP 客户端-服务器架构，以及 texlab、TeX 引擎、PDF 阅读器各自的角色
能说出 Home/Configuration/LSP-Internals/Previewing/Project-Detection/Tectonic/Workspace-commands 各页面的主题与建议阅读顺序
minimal_modules: LSP 客户端-服务器架构
lectures.practice_task: 通读本 wiki 全部 7 个页面，画一张关系图描述「编辑器(LSP 客户端) ↔ texlab(LSP 服务器) ↔ TeX 引擎 ↔ PDF 阅读器」四者如何交互，并用一句话标注每个 wiki 页面对应 texlab 的哪一项能力。

lectures.title: 项目识别与根目录检测
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 如何从单个 .tex 文件发现整个「项目」（编译进同一文档的所有文件）——通过向上遍历目录树、解析 \input/\import 构建依赖树；以及通过 .texlabroot / Tectonic.toml / .latexmkrc / 根源文件四步优先级确定根目录。
learning_goals: 理解「项目」概念与依赖树（\input/\import）的迭代构建过程
掌握根目录检测的四步优先级算法及其各自的影响（输出目录、src/build 目录、latexmkrc 推断等）
理解为何必须确定根目录：TeX 引擎的 \input 等命令基于工作目录而非源文件目录
lectures.practice_task: 创建一个含 main.tex（带 \documentclass 与 document 环境）并用 \input 引入 chapter1.tex 的多文件工程；分别在父目录放置 .texlabroot、Tectonic.toml、.latexmkrc，预测并验证 texlab 会选中哪个目录作为根目录。

units.title: 配置基础：理解 texlab.* 配置模型

lectures.title: 配置总览：命名空间、类型与占位符
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 的配置模型：配置由 LSP 客户端持有、服务器通过查询获取；配置项统一在 texlab.* 命名空间下，每项有 Type 与 Default value，部分项支持 %f/%p/%l 占位符。
learning_goals: 理解配置由 LSP 客户端持有、服务器查询的机制
学会阅读每个配置项的 Type / Default value / Placeholders 三要素
掌握 %f（TeX 文件路径）、%p（PDF 路径）、%l（行号）三个占位符的含义与出现位置
lectures.practice_task: 为你的编辑器写一份最小 texlab 配置 JSON，至少包含 build.executable、build.args（带 %f）、forwardSearch.args（带 %f/%p/%l），并为每个字段注释其类型与默认值。

lectures.title: 构建配置 texlab.build.*
lectures.topic: 逐项讲解 texlab.build.* 配置：executable、args、onSave、forwardSearchAfter、useFileList、auxDirectory/logDirectory/pdfDirectory、filename，以及已弃用的 texlab.auxDirectory 与 latexmkrc 自动推断的关系。
learning_goals: 掌握切换构建工具（latexmk / 其他）的方法与 args 中 flag 与参数需拆分为数组元素的约定
理解 onSave、forwardSearchAfter、useFileList 各自的作用与性能权衡
学会配置 auxDirectory/logDirectory/pdfDirectory 输出目录，并理解使用 latexmkrc 时 texlab 会自动推断
minimal_modules: 输出目录配置与 latexmkrc 推断
lectures.practice_task: 编写一份配置让 texlab 用 latexmk 把 PDF 输出到 ./build 目录、保存时自动编译；再说明若改用非 latexmk 工具，需要额外显式设置哪些目录项及为何。

units.title: 编译、预览与 SyncTeX 集成

lectures.title: 编译与预览的整体流程
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 编译文档的两种触发方式（textDocument/build 自定义请求与 onSave 自动构建）、forwardSearchAfter 如何衔接正向搜索，以及 SyncTeX 的角色；说明为何推荐 onSave 而非 latexmk 的 -pvc。
learning_goals: 理解编译的两种触发路径（自定义请求 vs onSave）及其区别
掌握 forwardSearchAfter 串起的「编译→正向搜索」工作流
理解 SyncTeX 在编辑器与 PDF 阅读器之间双向跳转中的作用，以及为何不用 latexmk -pvc
lectures.practice_task: 配置 texlab.build.onSave=true 与 forwardSearchAfter=true，在编辑器中修改并保存一个 LaTeX 文件，观察「保存→编译→跳转到 PDF」完整链路，并记录每一步由哪个配置项驱动。

lectures.title: SyncTeX 双向搜索与 PDF 阅读器集成
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab.forwardSearch.* 配置，并给出 SumatraPDF/Evince/Okular/Zathura/qpdfview/Skim/Sioyek 等阅读器的正向搜索与逆向搜索配置；介绍 texlab inverse-search 子命令及其与 window/showDocument 的关系。

能为自己平台上的 PDF 阅读器配置正向搜索（编辑器→PDF）
理解逆向搜索（PDF→编辑器）如何通过 texlab inverse-search 子命令回跳
minimal_modules: 各阅读器正向搜索配方
逆向搜索 / inverse-search 子命令

lectures.title: 使用 Tectonic 作为替代 TeX 引擎
lectures.topic: 讲解如何把构建工具切换为 tectonic（V2 与 V1 两种 CLI 写法），为何建议加 --keep-intermediates / --keep-logs，以及如何让 pdfDirectory/auxDirectory 与 --outdir 对齐；并联系 Project-Detection 中 Tectonic.toml 的根目录检测。
learning_goals: 掌握 tectonic V2（-X compile）与 V1 CLI 的 texlab.build.args 写法
理解 --keep-intermediates（补全章节编号）与 --keep-logs（上报编译告警）各自的作用
学会把 pdfDirectory/auxDirectory 与 tectonic --outdir 对齐，并理解 Tectonic.toml 对项目识别的影响

units.title: LSP 协议扩展：自定义消息与工作区命令

lectures.title: 自定义 LSP 消息：build 与 forwardSearch
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 对 LSP 的两处自定义扩展：textDocument/build（编译请求，返回 BuildStatus）与 textDocument/forwardSearch（正向搜索请求，返回 ForwardSearchStatus），以及用 experimental capability 声明这些可选请求的机制。
learning_goals: 理解 LSP 自定义请求/响应的扩展方式与 experimental capability 声明机制
掌握 BuildTextDocumentParams / BuildResult / BuildStatus 的结构与四种状态
掌握 ForwardSearchResult / ForwardSearchStatus 的结构与四种状态（含 Unconfigured）
lectures.practice_task: 假设你要为一个新编辑器实现 texlab 客户端，写出调用 textDocument/build 与 textDocument/forwardSearch 的请求/响应 JSON 示例，并枚举四种 BuildStatus 与四种 ForwardSearchStatus 分别在什么场景下出现。

lectures.title: workspace/executeCommand 工作区命令
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 通过 workspace/executeCommand 暴露的六条命令：cleanAuxiliary、cleanArtifacts、changeEnvironment、findEnvironments、showDependencyGraph、cancelBuild，以及「所有命令至多接收一个参数」的约定。
learning_goals: 掌握六条 workspace 命令的用途、参数类型与返回类型
理解 findEnvironments / changeEnvironment 与 EnvironmentLocation（name + fullRange）结构
学会用 showDependencyGraph 产出 DOT 依赖图、用 cancelBuild 取消 onSave 触发的构建来排错
lectures.practice_task: 在一个多文件 LaTeX 工程里依次调用 texlab.findEnvironments 与 texlab.showDependencyGraph，把返回的 DOT 依赖图渲染成图片；再调用 texlab.cleanAuxiliary 清理辅助文件，对比清理前后的目录内容。

lectures.title: 枚举映射：LaTeX/BibTeX 结构与 LSP 符号种类
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 把 LaTeX/BibTeX 结构（命令、环境、节、定理、方程、标签、BibTeX 条目等）映射到 LSP CompletionItemKind 与 SymbolKind 的规则表，及其在补全列表与符号面板中的呈现意义。
learning_goals: 理解 LSP 的 CompletionItemKind 与 SymbolKind 两个枚举的作用
能查表说明某类 LaTeX/BibTeX 结构在编辑器中以哪种图标/种类呈现
理解不同 BibTeX 条目类型（Article/Book/Thesis 等）被映射到不同枚举值的设计意图
lectures.practice_task: 取一段含 \section、\begin{equation}、\label、\cite 与一个 BibTeX 条目的 LaTeX 文档，在支持 texlab 的编辑器中打开符号面板与补全列表，对照映射表确认每项的 CompletionItemKind / SymbolKind 并截图标注。

lectures.title: 诊断、chktex 集成与诊断过滤
lectures.topic: 讲解诊断相关配置：chktex.*（onOpenAndSave / onEdit / additionalArgs，且不要重定义 -I/-f）、diagnosticsDelay、diagnostics.allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns，以及白名单先于黑名单叠加的过滤顺序。
learning_goals: 掌握启用 chktex 静态检查的三种时机与 additionalArgs 的注意事项（勿重定义 -I/-f）
理解 diagnosticsDelay 对诊断上报延迟与编辑体验的影响
学会用 allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns 正则过滤诊断，以及二者叠加时的执行顺序
lectures.practice_task: 启用 chktex.onEdit 并配置 additionalArgs，再用 ignoredPatterns 正则屏蔽一类常见告警；验证保存后这类诊断不再上报、其余诊断仍正常显示。

lectures.title: 符号、补全、悬停与 Inlay Hints
lectures.topic: 讲解与「编辑器内呈现」相关的配置：symbols.allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns / customEnvironments、completion.matcher（fuzzy / prefix 及其大小写变体）、hover.symbols（none/glyph/image）、inlayHints.*（labelDefinitions / labelReferences / maxLength）。
learning_goals: 掌握符号过滤与用 customEnvironments 把自定义环境加入文档符号（含递归过滤）
理解 completion.matcher 四种取值（fuzzy / fuzzy-ignore-case / prefix / prefix-ignore-case）的差异
学会配置 hover 符号展示方式（none/glyph/image）与 \label / \ref 的 inlay hint 及截断长度
lectures.practice_task: 用 customEnvironments 把一个自定义环境（如 mybox）加入文档符号；切换 completion.matcher 的四种取值观察补全结果差异；并开启 \label 的 inlay hint 验证显示效果。

lectures.title: 代码格式化：BibTeX 与 LaTeX
lectures.topic: 讲解格式化配置：bibtexFormatter（texlab / latexindent / none）、latexFormatter（latexindent / tex-fmt / none，注意 texlab 尚未实现）、formatterLineLength，以及 latexindent.local / modifyLineBreaks / replacement 与 latexindent 各 flag 的对应关系。
learning_goals: 掌握 BibTeX 与 LaTeX 各自可选的格式化器及其取值
理解 latexindent 的 --local / --modifylinebreaks / -r|-rv|-rr 三个 flag 与配置项的一一映射
学会设置 BibTeX 格式化的最大行宽（formatterLineLength，0 表示禁用）
lectures.practice_task: 分别为 .bib 与 .tex 配置格式化器（如 BibTeX 用 texlab、LaTeX 用 latexindent 并开启 modifyLineBreaks），格式化一份排版混乱的文档，对比前后效果。

lectures.title: experimental 扩展点：自定义命令与环境
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab.experimental.* 扩展点：mathEnvironments / enumEnvironments / verbatimEnvironments、citationCommands、labelDefinitionCommands / labelReferenceCommands / labelReferenceRangeCommands、labelDefinitionPrefixes / labelReferencePrefixes、glossaryReferenceCommands、followPackageLinks，以及「命令不带前导 \」等约定。
learning_goals: 掌握用 experimental 扩展自定义环境类型（数学/枚举/verbatim，后者可抑制诊断）
理解自定义引用/标签/术语命令的扩展方式与「无前导 \」约定
学会用 labelDefinitionPrefixes / labelReferencePrefixes 让自定义命令（如 \thm）产生的带前缀标签被正确识别
lectures.practice_task: 定义 \newcommand{\thm}[1]{\label{thm:#1}}，通过 labelDefinitionCommands + labelDefinitionPrefixes 让 texlab 识别 thm:foo 标签，并用补全或引用验证该标签可被引用。

data.head: bc7c636b3535c80821f6e50463375cea2a2790bd
data.rationale: 本仓库是 texlab（LaTeX/BibTeX 语言服务器）的官方 wiki，共 7 个页面，本身就是文档而非源码，因此讲义的“源码”即这 7 篇 wiki，实践任务以“写配置/搭集成/调用命令/验证行为”为主。学习顺序按“先建立全局认知，再深入配置与集成，最后理解协议扩展与可扩展点”设计：单元 1 用 Home 与 Project-Detection 让读者先搞清 texlab 是什么、如何识别一个 LaTeX 工程（这是几乎所有功能的前置）；单元 2 引入配置模型（命名空间、类型、占位符）这一后续所有讲义的公共语言，并聚焦最常用的构建配置；单元 3 围绕“编译→预览”主链路展开，涵盖编译流程、SyncTeX 双向搜索与各阅读器集成、以及 tectonic 替代引擎；单元 4 进入 LSP 协议层，讲清 texlab 的自定义请求、workspace 命令与结构→符号种类映射；单元 5 面向高级配置与二次开发，把 Configuration.md 中较零散的诊断/符号/补全/格式化/inlay hints/experimental 扩展点按主题聚合。讲义依赖遵循“先懂配置模型才能读具体项”“先懂项目识别才能讲依赖图与标签前缀”“先懂编译流程才能讲自定义 build 请求”等关系，避免读者跳读时缺失前置概念。Configuration.md 内容庞大（约 40 项设置横跨构建/诊断/符号/格式化等多个子系统），故按子系统拆成多篇，而非一篇过载。

lectures.title: texlab 是什么：项目定位与 wiki 导览

掌握根目录检测的四步优先级算法及其各自的影响（输出目录、src/build 目录、latexmkrc 推断等）
理解为何必须确定根目录：TeX 引擎的 \input 等命令基于工作目录而非源文件目录

learning_goals: 掌握六条 workspace 命令的用途、参数类型与返回类型
理解 findEnvironments / changeEnvironment 与 EnvironmentLocation（name + fullRange）结构
学会用 showDependencyGraph 产出 DOT 依赖图、用 cancelBuild 取消 onSave 触发的构建来排错
lectures.practice_task: 在一个多文件 LaTeX 工程里依次调用 texlab.findEnvironments 与 texlab.showDependencyGraph，把返回的 DOT 依赖图渲染成图片；再调用 texlab.cleanAuxiliary 清理辅助文件，对比清理前后的目录内容。

lectures.title: 枚举映射：LaTeX/BibTeX 结构与 LSP 符号种类
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab 把 LaTeX/BibTeX 结构（命令、环境、节、定理、方程、标签、BibTeX 条目等）映射到 LSP CompletionItemKind 与 SymbolKind 的规则表，及其在补全列表与符号面板中的呈现意义。
learning_goals: 理解 LSP 的 CompletionItemKind 与 SymbolKind 两个枚举的作用
能查表说明某类 LaTeX/BibTeX 结构在编辑器中以哪种图标/种类呈现
理解不同 BibTeX 条目类型（Article/Book/Thesis 等）被映射到不同枚举值的设计意图
lectures.practice_task: 取一段含 \section、\begin{equation}、\label、\cite 与一个 BibTeX 条目的 LaTeX 文档，在支持 texlab 的编辑器中打开符号面板与补全列表，对照映射表确认每项的 CompletionItemKind / SymbolKind 并截图标注。

lectures.title: 诊断、chktex 集成与诊断过滤
lectures.topic: 讲解诊断相关配置：chktex.*（onOpenAndSave / onEdit / additionalArgs，且不要重定义 -I/-f）、diagnosticsDelay、diagnostics.allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns，以及白名单先于黑名单叠加的过滤顺序。
learning_goals: 掌握启用 chktex 静态检查的三种时机与 additionalArgs 的注意事项（勿重定义 -I/-f）
理解 diagnosticsDelay 对诊断上报延迟与编辑体验的影响
学会用 allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns 正则过滤诊断，以及二者叠加时的执行顺序
lectures.practice_task: 启用 chktex.onEdit 并配置 additionalArgs，再用 ignoredPatterns 正则屏蔽一类常见告警；验证保存后这类诊断不再上报、其余诊断仍正常显示。

lectures.title: 符号、补全、悬停与 Inlay Hints
lectures.topic: 讲解与「编辑器内呈现」相关的配置：symbols.allowedPatterns / ignoredPatterns / customEnvironments、completion.matcher（fuzzy / prefix 及其大小写变体）、hover.symbols（none/glyph/image）、inlayHints.*（labelDefinitions / labelReferences / maxLength）。
learning_goals: 掌握符号过滤与用 customEnvironments 把自定义环境加入文档符号（含递归过滤）
理解 completion.matcher 四种取值（fuzzy / fuzzy-ignore-case / prefix / prefix-ignore-case）的差异
学会配置 hover 符号展示方式（none/glyph/image）与 \label / \ref 的 inlay hint 及截断长度
lectures.practice_task: 用 customEnvironments 把一个自定义环境（如 mybox）加入文档符号；切换 completion.matcher 的四种取值观察补全结果差异；并开启 \label 的 inlay hint 验证显示效果。

lectures.title: 代码格式化：BibTeX 与 LaTeX
lectures.topic: 讲解格式化配置：bibtexFormatter（texlab / latexindent / none）、latexFormatter（latexindent / tex-fmt / none，注意 texlab 尚未实现）、formatterLineLength，以及 latexindent.local / modifyLineBreaks / replacement 与 latexindent 各 flag 的对应关系。
learning_goals: 掌握 BibTeX 与 LaTeX 各自可选的格式化器及其取值
理解 latexindent 的 --local / --modifylinebreaks / -r|-rv|-rr 三个 flag 与配置项的一一映射
学会设置 BibTeX 格式化的最大行宽（formatterLineLength，0 表示禁用）
lectures.practice_task: 分别为 .bib 与 .tex 配置格式化器（如 BibTeX 用 texlab、LaTeX 用 latexindent 并开启 modifyLineBreaks），格式化一份排版混乱的文档，对比前后效果。

lectures.title: experimental 扩展点：自定义命令与环境
lectures.topic: 讲解 texlab.experimental.* 扩展点：mathEnvironments / enumEnvironments / verbatimEnvironments、citationCommands、labelDefinitionCommands / labelReferenceCommands / labelReferenceRangeCommands、labelDefinitionPrefixes / labelReferencePrefixes、glossaryReferenceCommands、followPackageLinks，以及「命令不带前导 \」等约定。
learning_goals: 掌握用 experimental 扩展自定义环境类型（数学/枚举/verbatim，后者可抑制诊断）
理解自定义引用/标签/术语命令的扩展方式与「无前导 \」约定
学会用 labelDefinitionPrefixes / labelReferencePrefixes 让自定义命令（如 \thm）产生的带前缀标签被正确识别
lectures.practice_task: 定义 \newcommand{\thm}[1]{\label{thm:#1}}，通过 labelDefinitionCommands + labelDefinitionPrefixes 让 texlab 识别 thm:foo 标签，并用补全或引用验证该标签可被引用。
